title: phlox carolina var. angusta
intro: Phlox carolina var. angusta, also known as Carolina phlox or narrowleaf phlox, is a low-growing perennial herb native to the southeastern United States. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and glycosides. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, phlox carolina var. angusta is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to possess a sweet, slightly bitter flavor and aroma.
